reasoning: Butterfly is a rigid property because an instance cannot cease to be a butterfly without ceasing to exist (it is essential). It supplies its own identity condition (+O) derived from biological species criteria and defines the unity (+U) of the organism as a discrete whole. It is independent (-D) as it does not rely on the existence of another specific entity to exist, unlike a role.

Red is anti-rigid (~R) because an object can change color without losing its identity (it is not essential to its instances). It does not provide identity conditions (-I/-O) because we do not identify 'a red' independently of the object that is red; it is not a sortal. It is non-unifying (-U) as it does not dictate the structural wholeness of its instances, and it is considered independent (-D) in this context because its definition doesn't require a specific external witness beyond the instance itself.

Food is anti-rigid (~R) because it is a role a substance or object plays; an apple can cease to be food (by rotting) without ceasing to be an apple. It does not supply its own identity (+O) because it can apply to liquids, solids, or aggregates (like 'water' or 'bread'), which have different identity conditions. It is anti-unity (~U) because any portion of food is still food (divisive), and it is dependent (+D) because it requires an organism/consumer to define its purpose.

Animal is a rigid property because an instance of an animal cannot cease to be an animal without ceasing to exist. It supplies its own identity conditions (it is a base sortal) and defines instances that are intrinsic wholes with specific unity criteria.

RedApple is a Phase because it is anti-rigid (~R); an instance of an Apple can change from Green to Red without losing its identity. It carries identity (+I) inherited from the Kind 'Apple', but does not supply its own Identity Condition (-O). It is unifying (+U) because an apple is an intrinsic whole, and it is independent (-D) as its existence does not require the presence of a specific separate external object.

A Group is a rigid property because an entity that is a Group cannot cease to be one without losing its identity. It supplies its own Identity Condition (usually defined by membership criteria or social cohesion), making it +O and +I. It is Anti-Unity (~U) because groups are aggregates or sums of members rather than single intrinsic wholes with a topological boundary, distinguishing them from individuals or singular objects.

A Country is a Rigid property because a specific country cannot cease to be a country without losing its identity. It supplies its own identity conditions (+O) such as geopolitical boundaries and sovereignty, and imposes unity (+U) as it is conceived as a single whole entity rather than a mere aggregate of land.

AmountOfMatter is Rigid (+R) because if something is an amount of matter, it cannot cease to be so and still exist. It does not carry or supply specific identity conditions (-I, -O) because matter is cumulative and divisible without preserving a distinct identity of individuals (one chunk of matter plus another is just more matter). It is Anti-Unity (~U) because substances are typically mereological sums rather than intrinsic wholes (no unified boundary). It is Independent (-D) as it does not require a specific hole or host to exist.

A PhysicalObject is Rigid because an entity cannot cease to be a physical object without ceasing to exist. it is +O because it supplies its own criteria for identity (spatio-temporal tracking) and is +U because a physical object is inherently a unified whole rather than an arbitrary sum of matter.

A GroupOfPeople is Rigid (+R) because if an instance ceases to be a group, it ceases to exist as that specific entity. It supplies its own Identity (+O) based on the membership criteria of the collection. It is Anti-Unity (~U) because, as a collection, its instances are mereological sums/aggregates of parts (people) rather than intrinsic wholes with a topological structure.

Being a LivingBeing is essential to all its instances (a cat cannot stop being a living being without ceasing to exist), making it Rigid. It provides a common identity condition and is a primary supplier of that identity (+O, +I). Living beings are intrinsic wholes (+U) and do not require the existence of another specific entity to exist (-D).

SocialEntity is Rigid because if an entity is social, it is essentially social within its ontological context. However, it does not supply its own Identity Condition (+O) or even carry a shared one (+I) because social entities can be individuals (Persons) or collectives (Organizations), which use different criteria for identity. It is Dependent (+D) as it requires a social system or agreement to exist, and Non-Unifying (-U) because it encompasses both unified wholes and non-unified groups.

Location is typically viewed as a rigid property (if something is a location, it cannot cease to be one without losing its essence), but it does not provide a common identity condition for its members (e.g., a GPS coordinate and a street address are identified differently). As it carries no identity of its own and can apply to diverse entities, it serves as a Mixin or Category.

Fruit is a rigid property because an instance of a fruit cannot cease to be a fruit without ceasing to exist as that entity. It carries Unity because fruits are considered discrete integral wholes with internal boundaries. However, it does not supply its own unique identity condition (+O) or even carry a unified one (+I) at this level, as the identity criteria (how to distinguish one from another) are typically inherited from more specific types like 'Apple' or 'Orange'.

Vertebrate is a rigid property because an organism cannot cease to be a vertebrate without ceasing to exist. It carries identity (inheriting it from the higher-level concept of 'Animal' or 'Living Being'), but does not supply its own unique identity condition separate from the genus. It implies unity as vertebrates are discrete, bounded biological wholes.

Caterpillar is a Phase because it is anti-rigid; an instance of a Butterfly (the substantial) is a Caterpillar for a specific period but can cease to be one while retaining its identity. It carries identity (+I) inherited from its substance sortal (e.g., Lepidoptera) but does not supply its own global identity condition (-O). It is a whole (+U) and exists independently of other external entities (-D).

Person is a rigid property because an instance of a person cannot cease to be a person without ceasing to exist (essentiality). It supplies its own global identity condition (Own Identity) to distinguish one individual from another across time, and it characterizes instances as intrinsic wholes (Unity).

LegalAgent is Anti-Rigid (~R) because an entity can cease being a legal agent (e.g., through disbarment or expiration of power of attorney) while remaining the same individual. It carries identity (+I) inherited from the underlying sortal (like 'Person'), but does not supply its own unique identity condition (-O). It is Unifying (+U) because it applies to discrete wholes, and Dependent (+D) because it exists only in relation to a legal system or a principal entity.

In the Guarino-Welty framework, 'Entity' is a top-level category. It is rigid (+R) because if something is an entity, it is an entity in all possible worlds. It does not carry or supply an identity condition (-I, -O) because identity conditions vary across its subtypes (e.g., an animal's identity differs from a rock's). It is non-unifying (-U) because it encompasses both wholes and aggregates, and it is independent (-D) as the most general ontological class.

Organization is a Rigid property (+R) because an instance of an organization cannot cease to be an organization without ceasing to exist. It supplies its own global Identity Condition (+O, +I) often defined by legal registration or formal charters. It carries Unity (+U) because it is a whole with internal structure and boundaries. It is Dependent (+D) because its existence typically necessitates the existence of members/agents.

Agent is classified as a Role because it is anti-rigid (~R); an entity (like a person or robot) can cease to be an agent without losing its identity. It is externally dependent (+D) because being an agent requires an action or a principal being acted for. It carries identity (+I) inherited from its base type (e.g., Person), but does not supply a unique global identity condition (-O) for all agents.

Apple is a rigid property because an instance of an apple cannot cease to be an apple without losing its identity (it cannot become a banana). It supplies its own identity conditions (it is a Type/Kind) and instances are considered conceptual wholes (Unity). It does not intrinsically depend on another specific object's existence to be defined.
